metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the base URL (or URL-derivation rule, e.g. a config key / env var) for deep-link anchors so the executor does not have to invent it.
findings.finding: The base URL used to construct deep-link anchors in coach output is never specified; an executor must invent or configure a URL that is not in the plan.
findings.location: What > Coach anchored deep-links bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add one line to the Coach anchored deep-links bullet: 'Base URL defaults to REBAR_DOCS_URL env var if set, else a relative file:// path to the guide; specify the default in a new config constant in src/rebar/config.py.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the schema change to `coaching[]` items (new field name, e.g. `guide_url`), the base URL source (hardcoded constant / configurable), and how `render_coach_notes` and `_render_plan_review_text` are extended to emit it.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that 'Pass-4 renderer (passes.py) + CLI text renderer emit a URL#anchor into the guide' but never defines: (a) the base URL of the guide, (b) whether it is configurable or hardcoded, (c) what schema change `render_coach_notes` / the `CoachNote` pydantic model undergoes to carry a `url` field, or (d) whether the existing `coaching[]` output array in `plan_review_verdict` gains a new field. This is an unspecified schema mutation on an existing LLM-output contract with downstream consumers.
findings.location: What section: 'Coach anchored deep-links: Pass-4 renderer (passes.py) + CLI text renderer emit a URL#anchor into the guide'; AC: '--help epilog URL added to review-plan; coach output emits stable anchored deep-links.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the canonical anchor format for each criterion section in the authoring guide (e.g., `## {CRITERION_ID}` → GitHub Markdown anchor `#{criterion_id_lowercased}`) so the parity gate and deep-link emitters derive identical anchors.
findings.finding: The plan specifies 'explicit heading anchor keyed by criterion id' for anchor-stability but never defines the anchor generation rule (e.g., `#f1`, `#criterion-f1`, `#F1`, `#criterion-id-f1`). Because the guide parity gate must verify that each criterion has a guide section, and the deep-link emitters must derive the anchor from the criterion id, this format is a load-bearing contract that must be defined before either component can be implemented consistently.
findings.location: What section: 'specify anchor-stability (explicit heading anchor keyed by criterion id)'; AC: 'a generator/parity check fails when a criterion lacks a guide section'.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'Each guide section uses `## {ID}` as its heading (e.g. `## F1`); the GitHub Markdown anchor is `#{id.lower()}` (e.g. `#f1`). The parity gate searches for `^## {id}` headings; the deep-link emitter constructs `{BASE_URL}#{id.lower()}`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the MCP error surface contract for `rebar explain`: specify whether errors are returned as structured tool-call error responses (e.g., `isError: true` with a code/message) or raised as a named exception type, and include this in the AC proof and unit tests.
findings.finding: The plan specifies error-state handling (unknown-id, malformed-registry, missing-file) and CLI proof (exit non-zero) for `rebar explain`, but does not define how these error states are surfaced on the MCP tool surface — whether as a structured error response, an exception, or a tool-call failure schema.
findings.location: What section: 'rebar explain <criterion-id>' bullet; AC: 'unit tests for all three surfaces'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the MCP error-return shape for each named error state (unknown-id, malformed-registry, missing-file) — e.g., `{isError: true, content: [{type: 'text', text: '...'}]}` per MCP spec — distinct from the CLI exit-code specification.
findings.finding: The MCP tool surface for `rebar explain` has no specified error-return protocol. The plan names error states (unknown-id, malformed-registry, missing-file) and specifies CLI behavior (exit non-zero, clear message), but MCP tools return structured error objects — not exit codes — and the plan gives no indication of what shape those errors take or how the MCP layer distinguishes them from a successful empty result.
findings.location: AC: 'rebar explain <criterion-id> works in CLI + MCP + library off one shared lookup, with defined errors for unknown-id/malformed-registry/missing-file'
findings.suggested_fix: In the AC or a new sub-section, add: 'MCP tool returns `{isError: true, content: [{type: "text", text: "Unknown criterion: BOGUS"}]}` for unknown-id; similarly for malformed-registry and missing-file. Unit test for the MCP surface asserts isError=true and message contains criterion id.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the MCP error-return protocol for `rebar explain` (what exception type or error dict each of the three error states produces on the MCP surface).
findings.finding: The MCP error-return protocol for the new `rebar explain` MCP tool is wholly unspecified. The plan names error states (unknown-id, malformed-registry, missing-file) and specifies CLI behavior (exit non-zero) but says nothing about what the MCP surface raises or returns for each state. The existing MCP tools use `raise ValueError(...)` for disabled-tool errors, but that pattern maps poorly to the three `rebar explain` error states, which require different semantics.
findings.location: What section: 'MCP tool' surface for `rebar explain`; AC: 'unit tests for all three surfaces'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `rebar explain` is wired as a pre-dispatch intercept (like `review-plan`/`criteria`) or via `_dispatch()` + `help/explain.txt`; the plan currently names `_llm_commands.py` but leaves the `__init__.py` dispatch wiring and `known_subcommands()` registration unspecified.
findings.finding: `rebar explain` is dispatched as a native-intercept subcommand (plan targets `_llm_commands.py`), but the `known_subcommands()` system in `_cli/_help.py` derives its set from `_cli/help/*.txt` package-data files — an `explain.txt` help file must also be authored, or `rebar explain BOGUS_SUBCOMMAND` will be rejected as 'unknown subcommand' before the intercept runs.
findings.location: What section / `rebar explain <criterion-id>` bullet; AC: `rebar explain BOGUS exits non-zero with a clear message`
findings.suggested_fix: Follow the pre-dispatch intercept pattern used by `review-plan` and `criteria`: add `if argv and argv[0] == 'explain': return _explain(argv[1:])` in `main()` before the `known_subcommands()` check. No `help/explain.txt` is then required, but a `--help` handler inside `_explain` is needed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` to the plan's edit targets — it needs a new dispatch arm (following the `review-plan` / `criteria` intercept pattern) to route `rebar explain` to the handler in `_llm_commands.py`.
findings.finding: The new `rebar explain <criterion-id>` CLI subcommand requires a dispatch arm in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` (the argv router where all subcommand intercepts live — e.g. `review-plan`, `criteria`, `verify-completion`), but this file is not named as an edit target anywhere in the plan.
findings.location: What section: 'rebar explain <criterion-id>: net-new across CLI (src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py)...'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What / file_impact section, add: 'Also edit `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py`: add an `argv[0] == "explain"` intercept arm (mirroring the `criteria` arm at ~line 418) that calls the new `_explain` handler in `_llm_commands.py`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_mcp_llm.py` to the plan's named edit targets for the MCP surface of `rebar explain <criterion-id>`.
findings.finding: The MCP tool for `rebar explain` must be added to `src/rebar/_mcp_llm.py` (where all LLM MCP tools live, registered via `register_llm_tools`), but this file is not named anywhere in the plan's edit targets.
findings.location: What section: 'rebar explain <criterion-id>: net-new across CLI ... MCP tool ... library export'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, change 'MCP tool' to 'MCP tool (add to src/rebar/_mcp_llm.py alongside the existing review_plan / verify_completion tools)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the error-return contract for each of the three `rebar explain` error states (unknown-id, malformed-registry, missing-file) on all three surfaces: CLI exit code + stderr text, MCP exception/return type, and library exception class.
findings.finding: The plan names three error states for `rebar explain` (unknown-id, malformed-registry, missing-file) but the AC test proof covers only the unknown-id case (`rebar explain BOGUS exits non-zero`). The behavior — error message text, exit code, return value — for 'malformed registry' and 'missing backing file' is unspecified on all three surfaces (CLI, MCP, library), making it impossible to write the unit tests the AC demands.
findings.location: AC: 'rebar explain <criterion-id> works in CLI + MCP + library off one shared lookup, with defined errors for unknown-id/malformed-registry/missing-file. Proof: `rebar explain F1` prints the section; `rebar explain BOGUS` exits non-zero with a clear message; unit tests for all three surfaces.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the guide's base URL is resolved for deep-links: name the config key (or hardcoded constant), its default value, and the fallback behavior when the URL is absent/unconfigured (e.g. emit criterion-id-only reference instead of a URL).
findings.finding: The plan does not specify how the base URL for deep-links is resolved (hardcoded docs URL, a new config key, or repo-local path), leaving the implementer to make an arbitrary choice that may break in offline or air-gapped environments and yields an unstable output format.
findings.location: What section — 'Coach anchored deep-links' / AC: '--help epilog URL added to review-plan; coach output emits stable anchored deep-links'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a config key (e.g. `[tool.rebar.docs].authoring_guide_url`, defaulting to `None`) so `_render_plan_review_text` and the Pass-4 renderer emit the URL only when configured, and fall back to a plain criterion-id reference otherwise. This keeps the feature opt-in for hosted environments and safe offline.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the shared library function (module + signature) that `rebar explain` CLI, MCP, and library all call, and specify whether it delegates to `registry.by_id()` + `get_prompt()` or introduces a new entry point.
findings.finding: The plan introduces `rebar explain <criterion-id>` backed by 'ONE shared library lookup the CLI/MCP wrap' but never defines the lookup function's name, module, or internal protocol — specifically whether it delegates to the existing `registry.by_id()` + `get_prompt()` machinery or introduces a new function. An implementer must invent this interface, and the three surfaces (CLI, MCP, library) may diverge.
findings.location: What section: 'rebar explain <criterion-id>: net-new across CLI (src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py), MCP tool, and library export. Define ONE shared library lookup the CLI/MCP wrap.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'The shared lookup is `rebar.llm.plan_review.registry.explain_criterion(cid, repo_root=None) -> str` — it calls `by_id(repo_root)[cid]` to resolve the descriptor and `get_prompt(...)` to retrieve the guide section text; CLI and MCP wrap this function.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add unit tests (for all three surfaces: CLI, MCP, library) covering the `malformed registry` and `missing backing file` error states explicitly named in the plan, verifying non-zero exit and a clear error message for each.
findings.finding: The plan explicitly names 'malformed registry' and 'missing backing file' as error states to define for `rebar explain`, but the AC test proof covers only the 'unknown id' case (`rebar explain BOGUS`). Two of the three specified failure paths have no corresponding test.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — rebar explain AC; also 'What' section: 'Specify error states: unknown id, malformed registry, missing backing file'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the AC proof line to: '`rebar explain F1` prints the section; `rebar explain BOGUS` exits non-zero with a clear message; `rebar explain F1` with a malformed registry exits non-zero with a clear message; `rebar explain F1` with the backing file absent exits non-zero with a clear message; unit tests for all three surfaces cover all four scenarios.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the known consumers of coach output (CLI, CI reporters, any downstream parsers) and state explicitly whether the deep-link URL is additive/opt-in or replaces existing text, and whether a `--no-links` or format-version flag is needed for backward compat.
findings.finding: The plan changes existing behavior (Pass-4 renderer and CLI text renderer now emit anchored deep-links in coach output) but does not enumerate the downstream consumers of that output format or address backward compatibility. The plan says 'note downstream consumers of coach output format' as an instruction to itself but never fulfills it.
findings.location: What section: 'Coach anchored deep-links: Pass-4 renderer (passes.py) + CLI text renderer emit a URL#anchor into the guide; specify anchor-stability … and note downstream consumers of coach output format'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge `src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py` as a consumer of `render_coach_notes`; clarify whether deep-link injection is plan-review-only (requiring a separate renderer or a flag) or affects code-review coaching output too.
findings.finding: `src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py` calls `render_coach_notes` and consumes the `coaching[]` output. If Pass-4 renderer changes to emit URL#anchor deep-links into `coaching` strings, this code-review consumer inherits the format change without acknowledgement in the plan.
findings.location: What section: 'Coach anchored deep-links: Pass-4 renderer (passes.py) + CLI text renderer emit a URL#anchor into the guide'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that the `rebar explain` shared library lookup delegates to `registry.by_id()` + `get_prompt()` (returning the live prompt rubric text) rather than independently parsing the authoring guide Markdown, to avoid a second metadata path that can drift from the registry.
findings.finding: The plan proposes 'one shared library lookup the CLI/MCP wrap' for rebar explain, but does not specify whether this lookup delegates to the existing registry.by_id() + get_prompt() machinery or independently parses the new guide Markdown file. If it independently parses the guide file, it introduces a second metadata path for criterion content that can silently diverge from the registry — a premature NIH abstraction when by_id() already returns id/name/scenario/checklist.
findings.location: What section — 'rebar explain <criterion-id>: net-new across CLI, MCP, and library export. Define ONE shared library lookup the CLI/MCP wrap.'
findings.suggested_fix: Implement the shared lookup as: `def explain_criterion(cid, repo_root=None): desc = registry.by_id(repo_root).get(cid); if not desc: raise UnknownCriterionError(cid); return desc` — the CLI/MCP wrap this. The authoring guide becomes a human-readable supplement (with deep-link anchors), not the primary data source for explain.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for the deep-link AC, e.g. a CLI invocation against a fixture plan that asserts coach output contains `#<criterion-id>` anchor URLs, runnable in CI.
findings.finding: The AC 'coach output emits stable anchored deep-links' has no proving command; there is no check that verifies deep-links are actually emitted in coach output.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria > '--help epilog URL added to review-plan; coach output emits stable anchored deep-links'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the AC: 'Proof: `rebar review-plan tests/fixtures/sample_plan.md | grep -E "#[A-Z][0-9]+"` exits 0 with at least one anchor; a unit test in test_passes.py asserts the renderer appends the anchor.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify an expand-contract sequence or rollback path for the coach output format change (e.g., add anchored URLs in a new optional field/section rather than mutating existing text, or gate behind a flag initially), and enumerate which downstream consumers must be updated.
findings.finding: The plan changes the coach output format (Pass-4 renderer + CLI text renderer gain URL#anchor deep-links) and explicitly acknowledges 'downstream consumers of coach output format', but provides no expand-contract sequence, opt-in flag, or rollback path for those consumers.
findings.location: ## What — 'Coach anchored deep-links' bullet; AC bullet '-- help epilog URL added to review-plan; coach output emits stable anchored deep-links'
findings.suggested_fix: Add URLs as a distinct, appendable block (e.g., a 'References' section appended after existing coach output) so the existing text contract is preserved; document the addition as additive-only in the AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit within-ticket delivery sequence: guide sections must be present (or stubbed with a passing sentinel) before or in the same commit as the extended parity gate, with a documented rollback step if the gate must be reverted independently.
findings.finding: Extending the CI parity gate (validate_packaged_routing or new check) to fail when a criterion lacks a guide section will cause CI to fail the moment the gate lands if guide sections are not added atomically in the same commit/PR.
findings.location: ## What — R-5 bullet; AC: 'Parity gate EXTENDED to cover the guide. Proof: ... fails on a missing/renamed section'
findings.suggested_fix: Merge guide skeleton (one stub section per criterion) and the extended gate in a single atomic PR; document that removing the gate requires also removing the guide-coverage AC so CI stays green.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify the guide authoring model: state whether the guide is (a) auto-generated by a script that must be run and committed, or (b) manually authored with the parity gate as a linter — and document the update workflow (e.g., `make generate-guide`) in a CONTRIBUTING or README section.
findings.finding: The authoring model of the guide (auto-generated from the registry vs. manually maintained with parity enforcement) is unspecified, leaving the update-ownership lifecycle ambiguous: it is unclear who triggers a guide update when a criterion is added or renamed, and whether the guide can drift between CI runs.
findings.location: What section: R-5 bullet ('Registry-derived authoring guide'); AC: 'a generator/parity check fails when a criterion lacks a guide section'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one behavioral test asserting that coach output for a known criterion id contains a correctly-formed `#<criterion-id>` anchor URL, covering both the passes.py renderer and the CLI text renderer.
findings.finding: The coach-output deep-link emission (new behavior added to passes.py and the CLI text renderer) has no test proof specified in the AC — not even a thin assertion that the emitted URL contains the criterion-id anchor.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — '--help epilog URL added to review-plan; coach output emits stable anchored deep-links'; also 'What' section: 'Pass-4 renderer (passes.py) + CLI text renderer emit a URL#anchor'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the AC: 'Proof: a unit test asserts that coach output for criterion F1 contains a URL ending in `#F1`; running with a renamed criterion id produces a clear failure or updated anchor.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider splitting R-3 (docs/plan-review-gate.md, no code) into a separate child task that can be merged immediately, independent of the R-5 guide and rebar explain work — the plan already identifies this as a candidate split.
findings.finding: The ticket bundles at least three independently-releasable outcomes with distinct actors: (1) R-3, a pure static doc for maintainers with zero code dependency; (2) R-5 guide + parity gate, a CI/authoring-tooling deliverable for criterion authors; (3) `rebar explain` CLI/MCP/library, an end-user feature. The plan itself flags the R-3/R-5 split ('may be split into two child tasks') but then adds a third independently-shippable feature on top. R-3 in particular has no dependency on any other sub-deliverable and could be merged immediately.
findings.location: Why section: 'R-3 (maintainer-facing invariant doc) and R-5 (author-facing guide + tooling) are DISTINCT deliverables with different actors and independent value -- may be split into two child tasks'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the deep-link implementation target from `passes.py` to the actual rendering site: `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/coach.py` (`render_coach_notes`) for Pass-4 coach output, and `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py` (`_render_plan_review_text`) for the CLI text renderer.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Pass-4 renderer (passes.py) + CLI text renderer emit a URL#anchor into the guide', naming `passes.py` as the Pass-4 renderer to modify for deep-link emission. However, `passes.py` only re-exports the coach mechanism from `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/coach.py` via thin aliases — the actual rendering function `render_coach_notes()` lives in `review_kernel/coach.py:162-185`. An implementer targeting `passes.py` for the URL-anchor change will modify the wrong file.
findings.location: What section — 'Coach anchored deep-links' bullet; `passes.py` attribution
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Pass-4 renderer (passes.py)' with 'Pass-4 renderer (`src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/coach.py` — `render_coach_notes`) + CLI text renderer (`src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py` — `_render_plan_review_text`)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py` as an edit target and specify which symbol (e.g. `explain_criterion`) will be added to `__all__` as the shared library export.
findings.finding: The plan specifies a 'library export' surface for `rebar explain`, but `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py` (which defines `__all__ = ['review_plan', 'claim_gate_check', 'registry_coverage']`) is not listed as an edit target. Adding the shared lookup as a library export requires editing this file.
findings.location: What section: 'Define ONE shared library lookup the CLI/MCP wrap'; AC: 'unit tests for all three surfaces'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the `Coach anchored deep-links` What-section description with the AC: either (a) add a sentence to the What section specifying which CLI command gets a `--help` epilog and define 'review-plan' as a named component, or (b) if '--help epilog' is part of the CLI text renderer work, say so explicitly so the AC proof step is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The AC line `--help epilog URL added to review-plan` names a deliverable ('--help epilog' on a component called 'review-plan') that is absent from the What section. The What section describes only 'Pass-4 renderer (passes.py) + CLI text renderer emit a URL#anchor into the guide', with no mention of a --help epilog or any component named 'review-plan'. An implementer following the What section would not know to add a --help epilog, and the AC proof step ('--help epilog URL added to review-plan') is unverifiable without knowing what 'review-plan' refers to.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — `--help epilog URL added to review-plan; coach output emits stable anchored deep-links`; What section — `Coach anchored deep-links` paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, extend the 'Coach anchored deep-links' bullet to read: '…CLI text renderer emit a URL#anchor into the guide. Also add the guide URL as a `--help` epilog on the `rebar review-plan` CLI command.' Then ensure the AC proof step says `rebar review-plan --help` prints the URL.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale for the URL#anchor deep-link approach (e.g. 'URL deep-links are chosen because MCP/IDE consumers can render them as clickable navigation — a plain criterion id requires a manual lookup step').
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for the chosen deep-link approach (URL#anchor into a hosted guide) over the simpler alternative of emitting a criterion-id reference in coach output — it only describes the mechanism and notes downstream impact.
findings.location: What section — 'Coach anchored deep-links'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific CLI command(s) (e.g. `rebar review-plan` / `rebar review`) whose `--help` output should include the epilog URL, and add a proving command (e.g. `rebar review-plan --help | grep docs/`) to the AC.
findings.finding: The AC '--help epilog URL added to review-plan' does not name which CLI command(s) receive the epilog, leaving the scope undefined.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria > '--help epilog URL added to review-plan'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the criterion-id→URL#anchor contract (anchor format, stability guarantee, consumers) in CLAUDE.md or a new ADR so future contributors know it is a public interface, not an implementation detail.
findings.finding: The criterion-id→URL#anchor interface is a new cross-component contract between the guide document, Pass-4 renderer (passes.py), CLI text renderer, and downstream consumers of coach output, but no ADR or CLAUDE.md update is planned to capture this contract's stability guarantees and governance.
findings.location: What section: 'Coach anchored deep-links' bullet; AC: '--help epilog URL added to review-plan; coach output emits stable anchored deep-links'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short ADR (e.g., docs/adr/anchor-stability.md) or a CLAUDE.md section titled 'Coach output deep-links' that states: anchors are generated as `#<criterion-id-lowercased>`, are considered stable once published, and must not be changed without a redirect or version bump; link to it from the guide and from passes.py.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `.github/workflows/test.yml` and `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` as edit targets if the guide parity check is a separate invocation (or confirm it is folded into the existing `validate-routing` call so no CI edits are needed).
findings.finding: Extending the parity gate to cover the authoring guide requires editing the CI workflow files (`test.yml` and `gerrit-verify.yaml`) that invoke `registry validate-routing`, but these files are not enumerated as edit targets or consumers.
findings.location: AC: 'Parity gate EXTENDED to cover the guide. Proof: `python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.registry validate-routing` (or the new check) fails on a missing/renamed section.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
